steps.title: Identify the roots and write factored form
steps.content:
The graph passes through $(8, 0)$ and $(-2, 0)$, so the roots are $x = 8$ and $x = -2$. Using the factored form:
$$f(x) = a(x - 8)(x + 2)$$

steps.title: Expand to find the coefficient b
steps.content:
Expand the product:
$$(x - 8)(x + 2) = x^2 + 2x - 8x - 16 = x^2 - 6x - 16$$
So:
$$f(x) = a(x^2 - 6x - 16) = ax^2 - 6ax - 16a$$
Comparing to $ax^2 + bx + c$, the coefficient of $x$ is $b = -6a$.

steps.title: Apply the constraint on a to find a + b
steps.content:
Compute $a + b$:
$$a + b = a + (-6a) = -5a$$
Since $a$ is an integer greater than 1, the smallest value is $a = 2$. When $a = 2$:
$$a + b = -5(2) = -10$$
For any $a > 2$, the value becomes more negative. The only choice that is achievable is $-10$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
